response: The internet evolved from a 1960s US Department of Defense project (ARPANET) to a global network now serving over 5 billion people. Key developments include the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP protocols, Tim Berners-Lee's invention of the World Wide Web in 1989, and the 1993 launch of the Mosaic browser that made it accessible to ordinary users. The dot-com boom of the late 1990s led to a 2001 crash, but survivors like Amazon and Google became major companies, while mobile internet after the 2007 iPhone launch and cloud computing (AWS in 2006) further transformed the web.
